title: Introduction to semicolons
description: Learn to identify and use semicolons in sentences!
topic_path: Punctuation: the colon, semicolon, and more

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the semicolon doing in this sentence?**

> Robin Hood stole from the rich$\redD{;}$ he also gave to the poor.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
Semicolons have two uses:

1. They can link related clauses in a complex sentence.

 *Alan missed the party because he was sick; Chloe couldn't make it either.*

2. They can separate items in a complex list (e.g., when commas appear in the list items).

 *I have lived in Chicago, Illinois; Nashville, Tennessee; and Long Beach, California.*
This semicolon is **uniting closely connected clauses.**

choices.content: It’s uniting two closely connected clauses.
It’s separating items in a complex list.
